eviction.notice_to_cure: 5 days for nonpayment (14 days for material noncompliance)

rentControl.details: Historical rent control existed but largely expired. No current rent control.
No statewide rent control. Montpelier enacted local just cause eviction (2024). Burlington has considered rent stabilization.
rentControl.local_preemption: no — local rent control not preempted

securityDeposit.limit: No statutory limit (but must be reasonable)

eviction.notice_without_cause: 60 days (30 days if tenant has lived there less than 2 years); landlord must have cause after initial lease term in some cases
eviction.just_cause_required: Partial — cause required during lease term; no-cause allowed at end of lease with proper notice (Montpelier has full just cause locally)

rentControl.details: HB 1217 (2025): Caps annual rent increases at lesser of 7% + CPI or 10%. No increases in first 12 months. 5% cap for manufactured homes (no expiration). 90-day notice required.
rentControl.local_preemption: yes — state law preempts local caps (but HB 1217 provides the floor)

eviction.just_cause_required: Yes — statewide just cause eviction (SB 5600, effective 2022). Landlords must provide one of 16 enumerated reasons.

rentControl.details: No rent control. State preempts local rent control.

eviction.notice_to_cure: 5 days for nonpayment (14 days for week-to-week)

rentControl.details: No rent control at any level.

eviction.notice_to_cure: No statutory cure period for nonpayment

rentControl.details: No rent control at any level.

eviction.notice_without_cause: No specific notice period required for lease expiration
